definition: The copper supply deficit is real but likely 2-4Mt cumulative by 2032 rather than the 5-8Mt projected by bullish analysts. Forensic analysis of 8 prior deficit forecasts shows 0/8 fully materialized at projected magnitude (20-30% hit rate). Brownfield expansion and scrap (30-50% of historical supply adjustment) and aluminum substitution work against the high end. Still sufficient to require prices 15-25% above current levels.
evidence: 20-30% hit rate at projected magnitude, upgradeable to 30-40% due to permitting delays
Brownfield expansion + scrap account for 30-50% of historical supply adjustment with 2-5 year response time
Pair_3 calibrated likely range downward to 2-4Mt
Still requires prices 15-25% above current to incentivize greenfield
0/8 major copper deficit forecasts fully materialized historically
Greenfield timelines extended from 5.8 years (1990s) to 12-18 years
Demand tracking: EVs ~1.2-1.5 Mt, grid ~0.8-1.2 Mt, data centers ~0.5-1.6 Mt
Aluminum substitution activated at $12,000+/t copper price
Three falsifications of fixed-coefficient mineral demand projections within last decade (platinum/diesel, cobalt/LFP, silver/solar)
open_questions: What is the price elasticity of aluminum-for-copper substitution?
Will permitting reform in key jurisdictions accelerate supply response?
Can the 2Mt vs. 8Mt gap be resolved by tracking Kamoa-Kakula, Quellaveco, Oyu Tolgoi ramp-ups?
Will copper break $12,000/t by 2027 (live falsification test)?
At what price does aluminum substitution materially reduce copper demand?
How much do technology coefficient changes reduce projected deficits?
